no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes requirements at a feature level (define an SPI, integrate with LocalNode, wire ServiceLoader, update BUILD files) without specifying exact code changes like line numbers or literal code snippets to insert.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact method name 'executeWithInterceptors' (test_execute_with_interceptors_method_exists), exact reverse for-loop pattern 'for (int i = interceptors.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--)' (test_interceptor_chaining_builds_from_last_to_first), exact field declaration 'private final List<NodeCommandInterceptor> interceptors' (test_local_node_has_interceptors_field), and exact variable naming 'interceptor.isEnabled(config)'. Alternative correct implementati

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost all f2p tests are purely text-matching via regex on source files (e.g., test_interceptor_has_isEnabled_method checks regex pattern in file content, test_interceptor_chaining_builds_from_last_to_first checks for a specific loop pattern in source). Only the compilation tests (TestBazelCompilation) actually execute code. The majority of tests are 'assert pattern in open(path).read()' style.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since tests only check for text patterns in source files (not actual execution), a stub file containing the right method signatures and patterns but no real implementation logic would pass all non-compilation tests. For example, test_interceptor_has_intercept_method just checks for a regex matching the method signature - an empty interface with the right signatures passes. The Bazel compilation tests do provide some guard, but most f2p tests are satisfiable by stubs.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires 'A single interceptor receives every WebDriver command executed through the node' and 'Multiple interceptors are invoked in the correct order' as behavioral verification, but no test actually executes commands through a node with interceptors. Only text patterns are checked.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into clearly named classes (TestNodeCommandInterceptorInterface, TestLocalNodeInterceptorSupport, TestLocalNodeFactoryLoadsInterceptors, etc.) with descriptive docstrings for each test method.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB outputs.

no_typos.reason: No typos detected in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact integration points like 'addInterceptor method' on the Builder, 'interceptors are stored in a list field', and 'A method wraps command execution' which are very specific implementation hints. It also names 'LocalNodeFactory' as the factory to modify. While it doesn't name the interface 'NodeCommandInterceptor', the tests require this exact name which creates a discoverability gap.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-addition task, not a config-edit task.
